Create comprehensive E2E tests for public user profiles and reputation.

## Test Scenarios

### Public Profile Page

- e2e/profiles/reputation.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/profile-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Public profiles render correctly
- Privacy respected
- Reputation accurately reflects

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","title":"Enhance copy button feedback with satisfying animation","description":"# Task: Enhance Copy Button Feedback

## Context
The copy button currently changes from Copy icon to Check icon when clicked. While functional, it lacks the "satisfying" micro-interaction that Stripe/Linear provide.

## Current Behavior
1. User clicks Copy button
2. Icon changes from Copy → Check (instant)
3. Toast appears
4. After 2s, icon changes back

## Proposed Enhancements

### 1. Icon Transition Animation
```typescript
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';

// In PromptCard.tsx copy button
<Button onClick={handleCopy} ...>
  <AnimatePresence mode="wait">
    {copied ? (
      <motion.div
        key="check"
        initial={{ scale: 0, rotate: -180 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1, rotate: 0 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0, rotate: 180 }}
        transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 500, damping: 25 }}
      >
        <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
      </motion.div>
    ) : (
      <motion.div
        key="copy"
        initial={{ scale: 0 }}
        animate={{ scale: 1 }}
        exit={{ scale: 0 }}
        transition={{ duration: 0.15 }}
      >
        <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
      </motion.div>
    )}
  </AnimatePresence>
</Button>
```

### 2. Button Background Flash
```typescript
// Add success background flash
const [showFlash, setShowFlash] = useState(false);

const handleCopy = async () => {
  await navigator.clipboard.writeText(content);
  setCopied(true);
  setShowFlash(true);
  setTimeout(() => setShowFlash(false), 300);
  // ... rest of handler
};

<Button
  className={cn(
    "...",
    showFlash && "bg-emerald-100 dark:bg-emerald-900/30"
  )}
>
```

### 3. Ripple Effect (Optional)
```typescript
// CSS ripple on click
.copy-button {
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.copy-button::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  background: radial-gradient(circle at center, 
    oklch(0.9 0.1 145) 0%, 
    transparent 70%
  );
  opacity: 0;
  transform: scale(0);
  transition: transform 0.3s, opacity 0.3s;
}

.copy-button.copied::after {
  opacity: 1;
  transform: scale(2);
}
```

### 4. Haptic Feedback (Mobile)
```typescript
const handleCopy = async () => {
  // Haptic feedback if available
  if ('vibrate' in navigator) {
    navigator.vibrate(50); // 50ms vibration
  }
  // ... rest of copy logic
};
```

## Design Rationale
- Spring animation on Check: Feels "bouncy" and satisfying
- Rotate effect: Draws eye, makes change obvious
- Background flash: Confirms action without being distracting
- Haptic feedback: Mobile users feel the action

## Reduced Motion Support
```typescript
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();

// Skip animation, just swap icons
{copied ? (
  <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-500" />
) : (
  <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />
)}
```

## Testing
1. Click copy - see icon spin/bounce into check
2. Background flashes briefly green
3. After 2s, icon spins back to copy
4. Mobile: feel haptic feedback
5. Reduced motion: instant icon swap
6. Multiple rapid clicks: animation doesn't break

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/PromptCard.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx (same pattern)
- apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx

## Dependencies
- Framer Motion (already available)
- Lower priority than functional improvements","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T06:09:39.419096088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:43.728049147Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:16:43.728049147Z","close_reason":"Implemented satisfying micro-interactions with spring animations, background flash, and haptic feedback","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1m9a","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T06:24:56.158090338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1qt5","title":"EPIC: Sharing & Collaboration","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-oam.

## Overview
Sharing enables users to distribute their prompts, packs, and collections with others - either publicly via the Swap Meet or privately via direct links.

## Sharing Modes

### 1. Public Sharing (Swap Meet)
- Make content visible to all premium users
- Appears in community browse/search
- Receives ratings and usage stats
- Builds author reputation

### 2. Private Link Sharing
- Generate unique shareable URL
- Anyone with link can view (no auth required for viewing)
- Track link views/uses
- Revoke link anytime
- Optional: password protection
- Optional: expiration date

### 3. Team/Workspace Sharing (V2)
- Share with specific team members
- Workspace-level shared library
- Role-based permissions (view/edit/admin)
- Activity feed for shared content

## Key Features

### Share Dialog
- Choose sharing mode
- Generate/copy share link
- Set link options (password, expiry)
- Preview shared view
- Social share buttons (Twitter, LinkedIn)

### Manage Shared Content
- My Shared Items dashboard
- View share statistics
- Edit sharing settings
- Revoke access
- See who accessed (for team shares)

### Receive Shared Content
- View shared content via link
- Save to own library (if premium)
- Fork/duplicate to own account
- Rate and review (if public)

### Share Targets
- Individual prompts
- Collections
- Prompt packs
- Skills

## Database Schema
- share_links (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, link_code, password_hash, expires_at, created_at)
- share_link_views (link_id, viewer_id, viewed_at, ip_hash)
- team_shares (id, content_type, content_id, team_id, permission_level, shared_by, shared_at)

## Security Considerations
- Rate limit link generation
- Obfuscated link codes (not sequential)
- Optional password protection
- View logging for audit
- DMCA/abuse reporting for public shares

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-1w92","title":"E2E Tests: Following and Activity Feed","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ypuk.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the following system and activity feed.

## Test Scenarios
1. **Following Users**
   - Follow button works
   - Unfollow works
   - Following count updates
   - Followers count updates

2. **Activity Feed**
   - Feed shows followed users' activity
   - New posts appear in real-time (or on refresh)
   - Empty feed message
   - Load more pagination

3. **Activity Types**
   - New prompt published
   - Prompt updated
   - Collection shared
   - User joined (optional)

4. **Filtering**
   - Filter by activity type
   - Date range filter
   - Hide own activity option

5. **Notifications**
   - New follower notification
   - Can toggle follow notifications

## Logging Requirements
- Log follow/unfollow actions
- Log feed loads and pagination
- Log filter changes
- Screenshot feed states

## Files to Create
- e2e/social/following.spec.ts
- e2e/social/activity-feed.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/social-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- Following system works reliably
- Feed shows correct content
- Real-time or timely updates
- Good empty states

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-2uex","title":"Build public user profile pages","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2h8.

## Task
Build public user profile pages.

## Page: /user/[username]

### Profile Header
- Avatar (large)
- Display name
- Username (@handle)
- Bio
- Join date ("Member since Jan 2024")
- Reputation badges (inline)
- Social links (icons)
- Stats: X prompts, Y saves received

### Content Tabs
- **Prompts** (default): Grid of public prompts
- **Packs**: Public prompt packs
- **Skills**: Public skills

Each tab shows:
- Grid of content cards
- Sort options (newest, popular)
- Empty state if none

### Features
- Follow button (V2 - placeholder for now)
- Report user button (in menu)
- Share profile button

## Database Updates
Add to user_profiles:
- username (unique, lowercase, alphanumeric + underscore)
- is_public (default true)

## API Endpoint
GET /api/users/[username]
Returns:
- Profile data
- Public content counts
- Badges

GET /api/users/[username]/prompts
Paginated public prompts.

## Username Validation
- 3-20 characters
- Lowercase letters, numbers, underscores
- Cannot start with number
- No reserved words (admin, api, etc.)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-30k9","title":"Build marketing landing page","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-6ax.

## Task
Build the marketing landing page for organic discovery.

## Page: / (root)

### Hero Section
- Large headline: "The Prompt Library for AI Power Users"
- Subheadline: "Discover, create, and share prompts for Claude, GPT, and beyond."
- Primary CTA: "Get Started Free" or "Try Premium"
- Secondary: "Explore Prompts"
- Hero visual: Screenshot or animation of product

### Features Grid
6-8 feature cards:
1. Swap Meet - Community marketplace
2. Prompt Packs - Curated bundles
3. Skills - AI agent configurations
4. Collections - Organize your library
5. CLI Integration - Terminal access
6. Analytics - Track performance

### How It Works
Three-step visual:
1. Sign up with Google (instant)
2. Browse or create prompts
3. Share and track usage

### Testimonials (placeholder)
- Quote cards (can be placeholder initially)
- Profile photos and names

### Pricing Preview
- Side-by-side Free vs Premium
- "View full pricing" link

### Final CTA
- "Ready to level up your AI workflow?"
- Sign up button

### Footer
- Product links (Features, Pricing, Docs)
- Legal links (Terms, Privacy, Guidelines)
- Social links
- Copyright

## Implementation
- Server component for SEO
- Animations with Framer Motion
- Responsive grid layouts
- Lazy load images
- Focus on Core Web Vitals

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-32fx","title":"Implement public roadmap and feature voting","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-zuri.

## Task
Create a public roadmap with feature voting to engage community.

## Why This Matters
- Transparency about direction
- Community input on priorities
- Reduce duplicate feature requests
- Build anticipation

## Features

### 1. Public Roadmap
- Planned features list
- Current development status
- Recently shipped features
- Timeline (quarters, not dates)

### 2. Feature Voting
- Upvote features
- Add comments
- Submit new ideas
- Track your submissions

### 3. Status Categories
| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Under Review | New submission |
| Planned | Will be built |
| In Progress | Currently building |
| Shipped | Live in production |
| Declined | Won't build (with reason) |

### 4. User Submissions
- Submit feature idea
- Add description
- Add use case
- Similar feature detection

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const featureRequests = pgTable('feature_requests', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // Content
  title: text('title').notNull(),
  description: text('description').notNull(),
  useCase: text('use_case'),
  
  // Status
  status: text('status').default('under_review'),
  statusNote: text('status_note'),
  
  // Tracking
  submittedBy: uuid('submitted_by').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  voteCount: integer('vote_count').default(0),
  commentCount: integer('comment_count').default(0),
  
  // Timeline
  plannedQuarter: text('planned_quarter'), // e.g., 'Q2 2026'
  shippedAt: timestamp('shipped_at'),
  
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const featureVotes = pgTable('feature_votes', {
  featureId: uuid('feature_id').references(() => featureRequests.id).notNull(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  primaryKey({ columns: [table.featureId, table.userId] }),
]);
```

## Public Pages
- /roadmap - Main roadmap view
- /roadmap/[id] - Feature detail
- /roadmap/submit - Submit idea

## Admin Features
- Change feature status
- Set planned quarter
- Merge duplicates
- Respond to requests

## Notifications
- Status change notification
- Shipped notification
- Vote milestone (10, 50, 100)

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/roadmap/page.tsx
- src/app/(public)/roadmap/[id]/page.tsx
- src/app/api/roadmap/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/admin/roadmap/page.tsx
- src/components/roadmap/

## Success Criteria
- Roadmap publicly accessible
- Voting works correctly
- Submissions moderated
- Status updates communicated

3. **Feature Announcements**
   - Banner for major features

   - Dismiss persists
   - Links work

4. **RSS/Atom Feed**
   - Feed URL accessible
   - Feed content valid
   - New entries appear

## Logging Requirements
- Log changelog views
- Log what's new interactions
- Log banner dismissals
- Screenshot modal states

## Files to Create

- e2e/changelog/whats-new.spec.ts

- Users stay informed
- Not intrusive

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-sqv4","title":"Add missing ARIA labels throughout the application","description":"# Task: Add Missing ARIA Labels

## Context
Several interactive elements lack accessible names, making them invisible or confusing to screen reader users. This is a WCAG 2.1 AA requirement.

## Specific Fixes

### 1. page.tsx - Clear Filters Button (line 166-171)
```tsx
// Before
<button onClick={clearFilters} className="...">
  Clear all filters
</button>

// After
<button 
  onClick={clearFilters} 
  className="..."
  aria-label="Clear all active filters"
>
  Clear all filters
</button>
```

Actually, since the button has visible text, aria-label is redundant. But we should add context:
```tsx
<button 
  onClick={clearFilters} 
  className="..."
  aria-describedby="active-filter-description"
>
  Clear all filters
</button>

{/* Add hidden description */}
<span id="active-filter-description" className="sr-only">
  {filterCount} filter{filterCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''} currently active
</span>
```

### 2. SpotlightSearch - Category Pills (lines 517-543)
```tsx
// Before
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() => setSelectedCategory(null)}
  className={cn(...)}
>
  All
</button>

// After
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() => setSelectedCategory(null)}
  className={cn(...)}
  aria-pressed={!selectedCategory}
  aria-label="Filter by all categories"
>
  All
</button>

// For individual categories:
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() => setSelectedCategory(...)}
  className={cn(...)}
  aria-pressed={selectedCategory === category}
  aria-label={`Filter by ${category}`}
>
  {category}
</button>
```

### 3. BasketSidebar - Remove Buttons (lines 223-229)
```tsx
// Before
<Button variant="ghost" size="icon" className="h-7 w-7 shrink-0">
  <X className="h-3 w-3" />
</Button>

// After
<Button 
  variant="ghost" 
  size="icon" 
  className="h-7 w-7 shrink-0"
  aria-label={`Remove "${prompt.title}" from basket`}
>
  <X className="h-3 w-3" aria-hidden="true" />
</Button>
```

### 4. BasketSidebar - Action Buttons (lines 240-277)
Ensure all action buttons have aria-labels for clarity:
```tsx
<Button ... aria-label="Download selected prompts as markdown files">
  <FileText className="h-4 w-4" aria-hidden="true" />
  Download as Markdown
</Button>

<Button ... aria-label="Download selected prompts as Claude Code skills">
  <Package className="h-4 w-4" aria-hidden="true" />
  Download as Skills ZIP
</Button>
```

### 5. SpotlightSearch - Semantic Toggle (lines 480-494)
```tsx
// Before
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() => setSemanticMode(!semanticMode)}
  title={semanticMode ? "Semantic search enabled" : "Enable semantic search"}
  className={cn(...)}
>
  <SparklesIcon ... />
</button>

// After
<button
  type="button"
  onClick={() => setSemanticMode(!semanticMode)}
  aria-pressed={semanticMode}
  aria-label={semanticMode ? "Disable semantic search (currently enabled)" : "Enable semantic search"}
  className={cn(...)}
>
  <SparklesIcon aria-hidden="true" ... />
</button>
```

### 6. SpotlightSearch - Clear Recent Searches (line 567)
```tsx
// Before
<button onClick={clearRecentSearches} className="...">
  Clear
</button>

// After
<button 
  onClick={clearRecentSearches} 
  className="..."
  aria-label="Clear recent search history"
>
  Clear
</button>
```

## Pattern: Icons Need aria-hidden
All decorative icons should have `aria-hidden="true"` to prevent screen readers from announcing them:
```tsx
<Copy className="w-4 h-4" aria-hidden="true" />
```

## Testing
1. Use VoiceOver (macOS): Cmd+F5 to enable
2. Navigate with Tab key - hear all buttons announced
3. Use VO+Space to activate buttons
4. Verify aria-pressed is announced for toggles
5. Test with NVDA on Windows if possible

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/app/page.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx

## Dependencies
- Should be done alongside other changes to these files
- aria-hidden on icons can be done with color fixes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:42:18.190269721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T14:46:18.776793960Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T14:46:18.776793960Z","close_reason":"Added ARIA labels and aria-hidden to SpotlightSearch (category pills, clear button, recent searches, featured prompts, icons) and BasketSidebar (close button, remove buttons, action buttons, decorative icons). TypeScript checks pass.","compaction_level":0}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t4lf","title":"Set up i18n framework for multi-language support","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xhrb. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Set up internationalization framework for future multi-language support.

## Why This Matters
- Global market expansion
- Non-English users
- Better accessibility
- Future-proofing

## Implementation

### 1. i18n Library Setup
Using next-intl for Next.js App Router:
```typescript
// next.config.js
const withNextIntl = require('next-intl/plugin')('./src/i18n.ts');

module.exports = withNextIntl({
  // other config
});
```

### 2. Message Files Structure
```
messages/
├── en.json       # English (default)
├── es.json       # Spanish
├── fr.json       # French
├── de.json       # German
├── ja.json       # Japanese
└── zh.json       # Chinese
```

### 3. Message Format
```json
{
  "common": {
    "save": "Save",
    "cancel": "Cancel",
    "delete": "Delete",
    "loading": "Loading..."
  },
  "auth": {
    "signIn": "Sign In",
    "signOut": "Sign Out",
    "signUp": "Create Account"
  },
  "prompts": {
    "title": "My Prompts",
    "create": "Create Prompt",
    "empty": "You don't have any prompts yet."
  },
  "errors": {
    "generic": "Something went wrong. Please try again.",
    "notFound": "Page not found",
    "unauthorized": "Please sign in to continue"
  }
}
```

### 4. Usage in Components
```tsx
import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl';

function PromptList() {
  const t = useTranslations('prompts');
  
  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{t('title')}</h1>
      <Button>{t('create')}</Button>
      {prompts.length === 0 && <p>{t('empty')}</p>}
    </div>
  );
}
```

### 5. Server Components
```tsx
import { getTranslations } from 'next-intl/server';

async function Page() {
  const t = await getTranslations('prompts');
  
  return <h1>{t('title')}</h1>;
}
```

### 6. URL Structure
```
/en/dashboard     # English
/es/dashboard     # Spanish
/fr/dashboard     # French
```

### 7. Language Detection
- Browser preference
- User setting (stored)
- URL path prefix
- Default fallback: English

## Database Changes
```typescript
// Add to user_profiles
preferredLanguage: text('preferred_language').default('en'),
```

## Initial Languages
- English (en) - Default, complete
- Spanish (es) - High priority
- French (fr) - High priority
- German (de) - Medium priority
- Japanese (ja) - Medium priority
- Chinese (zh) - Medium priority

## Implementation Phases
1. **Phase 1**: Framework setup, extract all strings (this bead)
2. **Phase 2**: Professional translation
3. **Phase 3**: RTL support (Arabic, Hebrew)

## Files to Create
- src/i18n.ts
- src/middleware.ts (locale handling)
- messages/en.json
- src/components/language-switcher.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Framework configured
- All UI strings extracted
- Language switching works
- Default English complete

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t524","title":"Implement API documentation for CLI and integrations","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-81ql.

## Task
Create comprehensive API documentation for CLI users and potential integrations.

## Why This Matters
- CLI users need API reference
- Future integration possibilities
- Developer experience
- Self-service troubleshooting

## Documentation Sections

### 1. Authentication
- OAuth flow for CLI
- Device code flow
- Token management
- API key usage (if applicable)

### 2. Endpoints Reference
For each endpoint:
- Method and URL
- Request parameters
- Request body schema
- Response schema
- Error codes
- Rate limits
- Example requests/responses

### 3. Common Patterns
- Pagination
- Filtering
- Sorting
- Error handling
- Rate limiting

### 4. Guides
- Quick start
- Authentication setup
- Common use cases
- Best practices

## Implementation

### OpenAPI Spec
```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: JeffreysPrompts API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /api/prompts:
    get:
      summary: List prompts
      parameters:
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of prompts
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/PromptList'
```

### Auto-Generation
- Generate from Zod schemas
- Keep in sync with implementation
- Version the API

### Documentation Site
- /docs/api - Main docs page
- Interactive API explorer
- Code examples (curl, JS, Python)
- Searchable

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/docs/api/page.tsx
- src/app/(public)/docs/api/[...slug]/page.tsx
- content/docs/api/*.mdx
- src/lib/api/openapi.ts
- public/openapi.json

## Success Criteria
- All endpoints documented
- Examples are accurate
- Kept in sync with code
- Easy to navigate


## Cross-Repo Integration
- Premium CLI endpoints to document: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o
- Premium device code auth endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1
- Premium token refresh/revoke endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve
